findings.title: tsc --noEmit gate does not type-check the test tree
findings.description: tsconfig include is ["src"], so the CI tsc --noEmit gate never type-checks test/ (including the named smoke deliverable). The tsc RBG correctly bites for src (planted TS2322 in src/__rbg_type_error.ts, since removed), but a type error introduced under test/ would slip past the typecheck gate and only be caught by bun test. Consider adding test/ to include so the gate covers the spine test it is meant to protect.

findings.title: Smoke test split across deliverable + auto-discovery shim
findings.description: The named deliverable test/smoke/spine-skeleton.smoke.ts holds the real test and runs correctly both via the explicit path (bun test test/smoke/spine-skeleton.smoke.ts → 2 tests, 1 file) and via the full suite; a one-line .test.ts shim re-imports it so Bun's default *.test.ts glob discovers it. Functionally sound and well-commented, but the doubled filename is a mild smell; folding discovery into the named file (or a bunfig testMatch) would remove the shim.

findings.title: Build-harness orchestration logic ships under src/ alongside the product spine
findings.description: src/orchestration/* (the smithering scheduler logic extracted for as-code testing, plus 7 self-test files) lives in the product source tree next to the walking-skeleton spine. It is deterministic, network-free, and usefully houses the SEC-1 secret scanner (scanForSecrets/redact), so it is not harmful, but mixing build-system code into the product src/ is an altitude/scope note for later separation. Does not affect the walking-skeleton seams.
